experiment: Native MCP vs OpenLakehouse Agent Coupling

approaches.description: Agent connects directly to Databricks MCP server or SQL connector
approaches.response_contract: Databricks-specific (Unity Catalog REST + SQL Warehouse schema)
approaches.agent_code_to_add_platform: Full agent rewrite or parallel integration
approaches.vendor_lock_in: High — agent logic tightly coupled to Databricks SDK/MCP schema

approaches.description: Agent connects directly to AWS Glue/Athena APIs or AWS MCP server
approaches.agent_code_to_add_platform: Full agent rewrite or parallel integration
approaches.vendor_lock_in: High — agent logic tightly coupled to AWS SDK/Boto3 schema

approaches.description: Agent connects to both Databricks and AWS MCP endpoints separately
approaches.response_contract: Mixed — agent must branch on platform at every step
approaches.agent_code_to_add_platform: New MCP config + new response parser + new branches per operation
approaches.vendor_lock_in: High — multiplied per platform; grows linearly with platform count

approaches.description: Agent connects to OpenLakehouse MCP server; receives Canonical Lakehouse Model responses
approaches.agent_code_to_add_platform: Zero — new adapter registered server-side, agent unchanged
approaches.vendor_lock_in: None — agent decoupled from all platform-specific schemas

zero_agent_modification_property.description: Adding a new platform adapter to OpenLakehouse requires zero changes to agent code, MCP tool names, or canonical response parsers
